content.full_text:
PLEASE REPLY TO

□ SACRAMENTO ADDRESS
STATE CAPITOL
ROOM 5202
(916) 445-5408
FAX (916) 322-3304

□ DISTRICT OFFICE ADDRESSES
1316 STATE STREET
SUITE 101
SANTA BARBARA, CA 93107
(805) 569-1725
FAX (805) 965-761

□ 801 SOUTH VICTORIA AVENUE
SUITE 200
VENTURA, CA 93003
(805) 654-4648
OJAI: (805) 646-8500
FAX (805) 654-4638

COMMITTEES

EDUCATION CHAIRMAN
NATURAL RESOURCES & WILDLIFE
VICE CHAIRMAN
BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS
ENERGY AND UTILITIES
BUDGET & FISCAL REVIEW
CONSTITUTIONAL AMENDMENTS

SUBCOMMITTEE ON OFFSHORE OIL & GAS
DEVELOPMENT CHAIRMAN
JOINT LEG-ISLATIVE AUDIT COMMITTEE

California State Senate

GARY K. HART
SENATOR
EIGHTEENTH DISTRICT

CHAIRMAN
EDUCATION COMMITTEE

August 11, 1992


Luisa Menchaca, Esquire
Legal Counsel
California Fair Political Practices
Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

BY MAIL & FACSIMILE

Dear Ms. Menchaca:

Please excuse my tardiness in sending you this
letter that you suggested to confirm the contents of your
advice to me made by telephone on May 7, 1992, regarding my
participation in legal education seminars this past few
weeks.

As I noted during our telephone conversation, I am a
senior staff consultant to Senator Hart assisting the Senator
on various legislative efforts, including his landmark child
support reforms which were signed into law this past year.

I am an attorney with expertise in family law matters,
and have also been an evening government policy instructor at
California State University, Sacramento.

Given my teaching skills and family law expertise, I was
invited to lecture at five legal seminars organized and
sponsored by the Rutter Group, a private for-profit
organization in Encino which puts on continuing legal
education seminars on a variety of subjects including family
law.

I first asked you whether it is permissible under state
law for attorney-legislative staffmembers with teaching
expertise like me to have my transportation expenses,
including air and auto travel, as well as meal and hotel
costs, renumerated by the Rutter Group during my
participation in these seminars.

Luisa Menchaca, Esquire
Fair Political Practices Commission
August 11, 1992
Page Two

Your response to me was in the affirmative, stating that
such travel and incidental expenses associated with my
participation in these legal education seminars may indeed be
paid by the Rutter Group to cover my transportation and
travel costs. These proper costs would include: air tickets
to and from the seminars; rental car costs to travel to and
from the seminars; meals consumed while participating at the
seminars; and incidental travel costs such as parking.

The second question I asked you was whether it is proper
under state law for me to accept reasonable renumeration for
my teaching efforts at the above continuing legal education
seminars. You again answered in the affirmative.

Specifically, you stated that State Code of Regulations
18932.2 and 18728 (a) make clear that it is indeed acceptable
for me to be reasonably compensated for my teaching at these
continuing legal education courses. You pointed out that the
courses information I presented needed to assist in the
improvement of the professional skills and knowledge of the
participants at the seminars and the course needed to provide
credit toward the continuing education requirements of the
attorney students present.

In my case these Rutter Seminars on Continuing Legal
Education in the Family Law did indeed meet these tests,
offering the attorney participants MCLE credit and presenting
them with up-to-the-minute education about the new family law
reforms passed this year by the Legislature.

I have been holding a compensation check received by the
Rutter Group for my teaching, Linda, until I could send you
this confirmation letter so you could confirm that the above
summary aply represents our earlier discussion.

Please call me today or as soon as possible at
916-445-5405 to confirm my understanding of our earlier
conversation, and, of course, of the state laws and
regulations we discussed.

Thank you again for your generous assistance with this
matter. I have always found the staff at the FPPC to be both
professional and highly competent.

Sincerely,

Drew Liebert

DRL:drl
